nodes.label: Desktop Guard-Signing Job (Developer ID secrets check)
nodes.rationale: `if:` conditions cannot read secrets.* directly, so the check is bridged through a job-level env: block and an output. When all five Developer ID secrets are present the signed+notarized job runs; otherwise the unsigned fallback runs. The two build jobs are mutually exclusive and the workflow is green either way.

nodes.label: Desktop Backend Base URL Injection (RIZ-76)
nodes.rationale: Injects RIZE_BACKEND_BASE_URL from the BACKEND_BASE_URL repo variable into a generated Config.local.xcconfig, which Config.example.xcconfig #include?s and which wins over the committed default; falls back to http://localhost:8080/v1 when unset. Read at runtime by UserDefaultsBaseURLProvider via the Info.plist key. Mirrors rize-mobile's RIZ-77 workflow, which solved the identical problem the same way.

nodes.label: Desktop Signed Build + Notarization Flow (Developer ID, hardened runtime)
nodes.rationale: Per the app's distribution model: Developer ID + notarization with hardened runtime, no App Sandbox — the Accessibility API is incompatible with sandboxing, so sandbox entitlements must never be added. Imports the certificate into a temporary keychain, builds with CODE_SIGN_STYLE=Manual, submits via notarytool, staples the ticket, and always deletes the temporary keychain.

edges.source_location: 'This mirrors rize-mobile's RIZ-77 release workflow, which solved the identical problem the same way'
'same bridging pattern as rize-mobile's release.yml guard-signing job / rize-backend's deploy-integration.yml guard job'
